cards.back: A traditional teaching method where the lecturer uses chalk to write on a blackboard and delivers a lecture to the students
A multimedia presentation that uses a combination of text, images, and multimedia elements to convey information to the audience

cards.front: Key characteristics of the chalk and talk method
cards.back: One-way communication, limited opportunities for student interaction, and the lecturer has control over the pace of the presentation

cards.front: Limitations of the chalk and talk method
cards.back: Does not promote active learning, may lead to student disengagement, and can be time-consuming for both the lecturer and students
Allows for visual aids, can be easily accessed and shared, and provides a structured format for presenting information
